rulings.Case Title: LUCIANO COSLOVICH et al VS. AMCHEM PRODUCTS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For A New Trial On The Order Grandting Defendant Hathaway Dinwiddie Construction Companys Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, APRIL 28, 2008 LINE 1. PLAINTIFFS LUCIANO COSLOVICH AND ELSBETH COSLOVICH'S Motion For A New Trial On The Order Granting Defendant Hathaway Dinwiddie Construction Company's Motion For Summary Judgment IS DENIED. THE RECORD REMAINS THAT PLAINTIFF HAS NO EVIDENCE THAT HATHAWAY DINWIDDIE WORKED ON A COUNTY BUILDING. A BID TO GET A JOB DOES NOT MEAN THE BID WAS ACCEPTED. PLAINTIFF HAS HAD OVER A YEAR TO OBTAIN THIS EVIDENCE AND HAS NOT DONE SO AND THE CASE IS SET FOR TRIAL ON THE DAY THIS MOTION IS SCHEDULED TO BE HEARD: THE REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE WAS MADE BASED ON A VIRTUALLY IDENTICAL RECORD WHEN THE MOTION WAS FIRST HEARD. =(302/PJM/VC)

rulings.Case Title: TANYA MURILLO VS. JEANNINE E CLARK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, APRIL 28, 2008 LINE 7. DEFENDANTS JEANNINE CLARK AND JAQUELINE BADENHAUSEN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED FOR FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. DEFENDANT'S MOTION FOCUSES ONLY ON WHETHER THE JANUARY 3, 2006 WRITING CONSTITUTES AN ENFORCEABLE WRITTEN AGREEMENT. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF'S SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT SEEKS TO ENFORCE BOTH A WRITTEN AND AN ORAL AGREEMENT THAT IS NOT BARRED BY THE STATUTE OF FRAUDS. UNDER THE DOCTRINE OF PAST PERFORMANCE, THE ORAL AGREEMENT MAY BE ENFORCEABLE. (1 MILLER & STARR, CAL. REAL ESTATE 2D (1989) SECTION 1:60 P. 168) THE MOVING PAPERS DID NOT ADDRESS THIS THEORY OF RECOVERY. =(302/PJM/PB)

rulings.Case Title: EDDIE KING et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Intervene
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, APRIL 28, 2008 LINE 8. INTERVENORS IVANA KIROLA AND ELIZABETH ELFTMAN'S Motion For Leave To Intervene. HEARING REQUIRED TO ADDRESS THE FOLLOWING ISSUES: (1) THE STATUS OF THE MOTION TO CERTIFY THE CLASS IN KING. (2) WHAT IS THE POSITION OF MORSE MEHRAN ON THE INTERVENTION? (3) THE STATUS OF MOTIONS IN THE KING CASE AND THE TRIAL DATE. (4) THE STATUS OF THE MOTION TO CERTIFY THE CLASS IN THE FEDERAL ACTION OF KIROLA, ET AL. (5) THE SCOPE OF THE PROPOSED COMPLAINT IN INTERVENTION AS ALLEDGED IN PARAGRAPHS 65, 69, 7O, 72, PRAYER FOR RELIEF PARAGRAPH 2A, 2C AND 2D. (6) WHAT ROLE DO THE INTERVENORS SEE FOR THEMSELVES IN THE KING CASE? IF THE MOTION IS GRANTED, DO THEY INTEND TO SEEK A CONTINUANCE OF THE TRIAL? =(302/PJM/AY)

DEFENDANTS RICHARD EDWARDS AND WINDWALKER SECURITY PATROL, INC.'S Motion To Set Aside Default And Default Judgment, And Leave To File Answer IS GRANTED BASED ON EXCUSABLE NEGLECT AND REASONABLE EFFORTS TO SEEK COUNSEL. =(302/PJM/AY)

rulings.Case Title: XXX VENTURES LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. LAURA GREENBERG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: SET FOR HEARING ON MONDAY, APRIL 28, 2008 LINE 15. DEFENDANTS LAURA GREENBERG AND SHERRY GENDELMAN'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. THE TENDER WAS NOT ACCEPTED AND THE REASON IT WAS NOT WAS NEITHER ARBITRARY NOR CAPRICIOUS. THE TENDER DID NOT DISCHARGE THE DEBT. C.C. 1500. THE TENDER SIMPLY PROVIDED DEFENDANTS WITH A DEFENSE TO AN UNLAWFUL DETAINER ACTION FOR NON-PAYMENT OF RENT. SEE RANDOL V TATUM (1893) 98 CAL 390, 398-99. =(302/PJM/PB)
